title: ZKP-Based Agent Auth for CI/CD Pipelines
description: GitHub's agentic workflows expose secrets via environment variables susceptible to prompt injection. Bolyra's handshake protocol could let agents prove authorization without ever holding raw credentials, replacing token-in-env with a zero-knowledge proof of delegated permission scoped to the specific workflow step.

title: ZKP Credential Middleware for LangChain Agent Auth
description: LangChain's sandbox auth proxies inject credentials via sidecar to prevent exfiltration but still rely on plaintext OAuth tokens at rest. Bolyra's existing LangChain integration could replace stored tokens with ZKP proof-of-enrollment, letting agents prove service authorization without the proxy ever seeing the credential material.

title: Cryptographic Delegation Narrowing for Multi-Agent Systems
description: CrewAI's allow_delegation flag is a runtime boolean with no cryptographic enforcement — any permitted agent can pass full authority to subordinates. Bolyra's Delegation circuit enforces one-way scope narrowing on-chain, giving CrewAI operators a verifiable guarantee that sub-delegated agents can never escalate permissions beyond what their parent was granted.

title: Zero-Knowledge Agent Risk Attestation for Entra Conditional Access
description: Microsoft's Agent Identity Perimeter evaluates agent risk at every resource touch but relies on centralized Entra telemetry. Bolyra could provide a privacy-preserving risk attestation layer where agents prove compliance with Conditional Access policies (model hash, operator identity, permission tier) without exposing the underlying credential to the identity provider.

title: On-Chain Spend Policy Enforcement via Cumulative-Bit Permissions
description: Oracle's evidence-and-control layer blocks agents that exceed budget thresholds, but enforcement is centralized and auditable only by the platform operator. Bolyra's 8-bit cumulative permission model (FINANCIAL_SMALL / MEDIUM / UNLIMITED) maps directly to tiered spend gates and can be verified on-chain, giving enterprises a tamper-proof, auditable spend ceiling that no agent or intermediary can silently escalate.

title: Privacy-Preserving Agent Identity Disclosure for EU AI Act Article 50
description: The EU AI Act mandates agent identity disclosure and interaction logging by August 2026. Bolyra's human-uniqueness and agent-policy proofs let deployers satisfy transparency requirements — proving an agent's operator, model class, and permission scope — without leaking proprietary model details or user PII to regulators or counterparties.

title: ZKP Handshake for Agent-Native Payment Authorization
description: Stripe identifies agent-native checkout as a key agentic commerce pillar but current flows rely on API keys or OAuth scopes that agents hold in plaintext. Bolyra's mutual handshake protocol could let a shopping agent prove it holds a valid delegation from a human (with a specific FINANCIAL tier) to a merchant's payment endpoint, without exposing the human's identity or the agent's raw credentials.

title: ZKP Layer for Non-Human Identity Lifecycle Management
description: Silverfort's Fabrix acquisition signals enterprise demand for unified human + non-human + AI agent identity. Bolyra's architecture already unifies human (Semaphore-based) and agent (EdDSA credential) identity under one proof system — positioning it as the cryptographic primitive layer that identity platforms like Silverfort could embed rather than build from scratch.

title: Agent Identity Infrastructure as VC-Fundable Primitive
description: With $300B in Q1 2026 VC and 63% going to AI, agent infrastructure is the hottest funding category. Bolyra occupies a unique niche — cryptographic identity primitive rather than application layer — that maps to the 'picks and shovels' thesis investors favor during platform shifts. The open-source SDK + on-chain verifier model mirrors the playbook that attracted infrastructure-stage capital.
